review_snippets.content:

Pros

G2 used to be on track to do great things.
It felt like there was a shared vision, a clear path towards growth (both personally and as a company) and overall just great vibes. Work was fun, the managers were fantastic and even in tough years we were doing well.
Then G2 started hiring leaders from upwork. Take a look at their Glassdoor reviews for AE's and you'll see what you're getting into.

review_snippets.title: All of the Changes Brian Niccol Has Made at Starbucks so Far
review_snippets.content:
## Starbucks updated its mission — and in-store policies

In January 2025, Starbucks updated its mission: "To be the premier purveyor of the finest coffee in the world, inspiring and nurturing the human spirit— one person, one cup, and one neighborhood at a time."

The update introduced several policy changes, including free in-store refills for brewed coffee and tea, the rollout of ceramic cups and cozy seating to encourage longer visits, and the reintroduction of Starbucks' policy of not allow

review_snippets.title: 5 Big Changes The New Starbucks CEO Has Made In 2025
review_snippets.content:
While "Back to Starbucks" may be all about returning to the chain's coffee shop roots, the road to that goal is paved with new technology. With an ambitious desire to roll out each order in four minutes or less, Niccol implemented a new way of organizing incoming orders, with a feature called Smart Queue. It's programmed to automatically prioritize certain tickets over others (mainly putting in-store ones first) and let the customers know the exact status of their order. [...] ## Slimmer menu

review_snippets.title: 5 Major Changes You'll See at Starbucks This Year - Eat This Not That
review_snippets.content: By the end of this year, Starbucks will finish adding new Clover Vertica brewers to all of its U.S. stores in order to streamline drip coffee service – the machine grinds and brews single-cup coffees in under a minute. The company is also changing drip coffee handoff: in a tweaked workflow, cashiers will give brewed coffees directly to customers, rather than sending the drink “down the line” to the service counter where other handcrafted drinks and mobile orders are picked up. CEO approval

Business Outlook

Pros

G2 used to be on track to do great things.
It felt like there was a shared vision, a clear path towards growth (both personally and as a company) and overall just great vibes. Work was fun, the managers were fantastic and even in tough years we were doing well.
Then G2 started hiring leaders from upwork. Take a look at their Glassdoor reviews for AE's and you'll see what you're getting into.
This is supposed to be pros so I will say the other IC's are great.

Cons [...] 5.0

Feb 26, 2026

Senior designer

Current employee, more than 1 year

Detroit, MI

Recommend

CEO approval

Business Outlook

Pros

Flexible, remote-friendly work environment that you can actually count on. The people here are great — collaborative, supportive, and genuinely fun to work with. Leadership provides the tools and resources you need to succeed, including solid investment in AI to help you build skills that matter.
Performance is recognized and entrepreneurial thinking is rewarded, which makes the work feel meaningful and motivating.

Cons

It's a fast-paced, outcomes-focused environment, so you have to stay on top of your game. Not a con if you're driven, but worth knowing going in.

See reviews by: Helpful|Rating|Date|All [...] Cons

I'm trying to be fair here but honestly leadership changes absolutely crushed the culture and forced good people out. Layoffs followed almost immediately by rehiring for the same role is a perfect representation of what's going on at G2.
The product has a meh value prop at this point and AE pay is mediocre at best.

## Explore other reviews about G2

5.0

Great teammates and culture. Atmosphere is friendly and supporting. Management and Leadership encourages learning mindsets, especially in the world of AI. Encourage trying new things, taking risks, etc.

Cons

Things move fast, sometimes very fast, occasionally without clear vision/purpose

[Starbucks (SBUX) earnings Q2 2024 - CNBC]: ## Waning sales

Starbucks' most dedicated customers have stayed loyal and been using discounts offered via the company's mobile app, executives said. But coffee drinkers who visit only occasionally have been buying Starbucks' macchiatos and cold brew less often, executives said; Narasimhan said those customers want more variety from their coffee.

Starbucks is planning to offer a version of its app that allows customers to order without being a loyalty member in order to attract these occasional customers to visit more frequently.

Narasimhan said Starbucks is also exploring how to meet overnight demand, from 5 p.m. to 5 a.m. The company conducted a pilot test, which Narasimhan said doubled business.

He also said the chain's lavender drinks were one of its most successful launches. [...] Net salesdropped nearly 2% to $8.56 billion.

For fiscal 2024, Starbucks now expects revenue growth in the low single digits, down from its prior forecast of 7% to 10%. The company also revised its projections for global and U.S. same-store sales growth to a range of low single digits to flat from its previous forecast of 4% to 6%. Same-store sales in China are expected to decline by single digits, down from the prior outlook of a single-digit increase.

Starbucks now also expects earnings per share growth in a range of flat to low single digits. It previously forecast its earnings would climb 15% to 20% in fiscal 2024.

The company forecasts that sales will start improving in the fiscal fourth quarter.

## Waning sales [...] The company's same-store sales fell 4% as traffic to its cafes declined 6% in the quarter. Wall Street was anticipating same-store sales growth of 1%, according to StreetAccount estimates.

Across all regions, Starbucks reported shrinking same-store sales and falling traffic.

In the U.S., same-store sales decreased 3% as traffic sank 7%. This marks the second quarter that the company's home market has struggled. Last quarter, executives blamed sluggish sales on boycotts targeting the company due to "misperceptions" of its stance on Israel.

Starbucks' international segment reported same-store sales declines of 6% as both average ticket and transactions dropped. In China, Starbucks' second-largest market, same-store sales plunged 11%, fueled by an 8% decline in average ticket.

[Starbucks Reviews | Read Customer Service Reviews of www.starbucks.com]: I have experienced Starbucks in many different countries and I have never encountered such an unhygienic situation or such a poor handling of a legitimate complaint. If you are serving products under these conditions, the very least you can do is take complaints seriously, inform customers transparently about corrective actions, and process refunds promptly.  
For me, Starbucks used to represent international standards and comfort. After my experience in Germany, however, it will remain in my mind as a brand that does not take hygiene seriously and refuses to take responsibility for its mistakes.  
If you are looking for a place where hygiene seems to be optional and where it is difficult to find a serious point of contact when a problem occurs, this is definitely the right place. [...] On September 27, 2025, I purchased two products from the Starbucks store at Frankfurt Zeil in Germany. While my products were being packed, a person who had been doing cleaning came and packed my order while still wearing the same gloves used for cleaning. Basic hygiene rules were clearly ignored. I was surprised at the moment, but since tongs were used, I did not immediately make a big issue out of it.  
However, when I got home and opened the product, I was shocked. There were water droplets on top of the mosaic cake and a large white hair on it. I immediately took photos and reported the incident to Starbucks Germany customer service. [...] ### Based on these reviews
